description:
Spec: docs/cps-in-clif.md §4 (Receive park/resume), §5 (Spawn entry).

Work:
- `fz_main_entry(initial_fn_ptr: *const c_void, halt_cont: *mut u8)` — SystemV entry callable from Rust scheduler. Implementation: a Cranelift fn declared at module-init time with SystemV CC that performs `return_call_indirect` into the initial fn with Tail-CC sig `(halt_cont) tail`. (For non-zero-arg entry fns, scheduler provides args; main is zero-arg by spec.)
- `fz_resume_park(msg: u64, parked_cont: *mut u8)` — SystemV entry. Loads `parked_cont+16`, `return_call_indirect Tail sig_cont (msg, parked_cont)`. Cranelift fn registered at module-init.
- Runtime/scheduler in runtime/src/runtime.rs:
  - `Runtime::spawn(closure)` deep-copies closure into new Process's heap, allocates halt-cont singleton in process heap, calls `fz_main_entry(closure.code, halt_cont)`.
  - On `state = Blocked` + message arrival, scheduler dequeues message, sets state = Running, calls `fz_resume_park(msg, parked_cont)`.
- Delete the trampoline `run_quantum` dispatch loop. `next_frame` field can stay as a vestigial sentinel or be removed; cleanup is fz-siu.1.13.

Acceptance:
- `concurrency_ping_pong.fz` runs end-to-end.
- §8.4 target clif for main matches: `main(i64) tail` with `fz_receive_park(v_k1)` return_call.
- `Runtime::spawn` no longer references frame_sizes for new tasks.
- Re-enable `concurrency_ping_pong_matches_cps_in_clif_section_8_4` full structural check (current GREEN test asserts only a subset).
title: fz-cps.1.11: fz_main_entry + fz_resume_park SystemV shims
